name: Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail
name_original: Ruta circular de la Ermita de San Miguel de Fuentespalda (PR-TE 158)
description: Hike the easy 2.6-mile Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail for panoramic views and a historic hermitage.
region_intro: Hiking around Fuentespalda offers diverse trails through a mountainous and rugged landscape in the Matarraña region of Teruel, Aragon. The area features rocky terrain, deep ravines, and expansive panoramic views, with the Pena and Tastavins rivers contributing to its rich geological diversity. It is situated where the Iberian System, Mediterranean System, and Ebro Depression converge, providing varied natural environments for outdoor activities. The region is also characterized by extensive pine forests, particularly Aleppo pine, and is close to Els Ports de Tortosa-Beceite Natural Park. Best hiking trails around Fuentespalda The most popular hiking route is Salt de la Portellada Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to the 20-meter Portellada Waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the town and the Tastavins river valley, culminating at a historic hermitage. Local hikers also love the Pena Reservoir – Pantano de Pena loop from Matarraña Nautic, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through scenic views around the reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Fuentespalda is defined by rugged mountains, serene river valleys, and diverse pine forests. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy family-friendly walks to more demanding trails. The routes in Fuentespalda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 2,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuentespalda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fuentespalda?
A: Fuentespalda offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with nearly 100 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking options in Fuentespalda?
A: Yes, Fuentespalda is an excellent destination for families. There are 23 easy trails, many of which are suitable for all ages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall loop from La Portellada&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km path that leads to the stunning Portellada Waterfall, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls in Fuentespalda?
A: The region is home to beautiful natural features, including waterfalls. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974451608/salt-de-la-portellada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Portellada Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.5 km hike that takes you directly to the impressive 20-meter Portellada Waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular walks around Fuentespalda?
A: Yes, many routes in Fuentespalda are designed as loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Pena – Pena Reservoir loop from La Rica&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3 km circular trail that provides scenic views around the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Fuentespalda?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Fuentespalda's trails often lead to or pass by significant points of interest. You can explore the historic Hermitage of San Miguel, which offers panoramic views of the town and Tastavins river valley. Another unique attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;, where you can follow the Matarraña river through a gorge with wooden sections. The region also features ancient sites like the Ruta de los túmulos ibéricos and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4623502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fenellasa Rock Paintings&lt;/a&gt;, part of a UNESCO World Heritage site.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Fuentespalda offers 18 difficult trails. The rugged, mountainous terrain, especially near Els Ports de Tortosa-Beceite, provides demanding routes with significant elevation changes and expansive views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fuentespalda?
A: The hiking routes in Fuentespalda are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from rocky ravines to serene river valleys, and the well-maintained network of trails that immerse visitors in the region's natural and historical tapestry.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Fuentespalda?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in Spain, including those around Fuentespalda, are generally dog-friendly, especially on marked trails. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, particularly in areas like Els Ports de Tortosa-Beceite, which is a National Reserve. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities besides hiking?
A: Absolutely! Fuentespalda is a hub for various outdoor pursuits. Besides hiking, you can enjoy mountain biking, horseback riding, rock climbing, 4x4 expeditions, canyoning, and bird watching. For an adrenaline rush, the town even features one of Europe's longest zip lines, offering breathtaking views over the Matarraña landscape.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the trails around Fuentespalda?
A: The scenery around Fuentespalda is incredibly diverse and striking. You'll encounter mountainous and rugged terrain with rocky landscapes, deep ravines, and expansive panoramic views. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, particularly Aleppo pine, and is shaped by the convergence of the Iberian System, Mediterranean System, and Ebro Depression, creating a rich geological tapestry. The Pena and Tastavins rivers also add serene river valleys to the mix.

Q: Are there trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Fuentespalda provide spectacular viewpoints due to the region's mountainous and rugged terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981174546/hermitage-of-san-miguel-de-fuentespalda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, culminates at a historic hermitage offering panoramic views of the town and the Tastavins river valley. Other routes in the higher elevations, especially those bordering Els Ports de Tortosa-Beceite, also promise expansive vistas.
tips: From the Hermitage you will have beautiful views of the entire town of Fuentespalda and the valley of the Tastavins river. The construction of the Hermitage dates from the year 1787 as indicated on its main façade and as usually happens in many of this type, a calvary is preserved that runs along the ascent path to it and in this case a very picturesque one , surrounded by beautiful cypress trees.
Curious statue next to the fountain known as the Lavadero Fuente de la Villa. Its history is currently unknown but it is undoubtedly one of the attractions of the town of Fuentespalda.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 4.17 km long and generally takes about 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete. It's considered an easy hike, suitable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily accessible paths. While there is a noticeable rise towards the hermitage, it's managed by a zigzag path, making it not overly difficult. You'll traverse agricultural fields with cereals, grapevines, and almond trees, and also pass through areas of thick pine trees.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks and views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest. The most prominent is the historic Ermita de San Miguel (Fuentespalda viewpoint), dating back to 1787, which offers beautiful panoramic views of Fuentespalda. You'll also see the Fuente de la Villa Washhouse, a preserved calvary among cypress trees on the ascent, and enjoy picturesque vistas of the Tastavins River valley and the town's medieval lookout tower.

faqs.question: Is the Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is considered an easy hike, suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families. The paths are easily accessible, and the elevation gain is moderate, making it a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs are not provided in the available information. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding leash requirements or restrictions before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming fields or colorful foliage. Summers can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon hike is advisable then.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail (PR-TE 158).

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided, but as the trail is near Fuentespalda, it's generally accessible from the town. Look for designated parking areas within Fuentespalda or near the start of the PR-TE 158 route.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails, including the Senda del Maestrazgo and the PR-TE 158 Ruta de la Ermita de San Miguel de Fuentespalda / Ruta de l'Ermita de Sant Miquel de Fontdespatla, as well as the Camino de la Muela.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail is a circular route, meaning you will end up back near your starting point without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the town of Fuentespalda. While specific establishments on the trail itself are not mentioned, you can expect to find cafes and restaurants within the town for refreshments before or after your hike.
